{{Quote|We're not heading to Korriban just yet. I just received a distress signal Rosh [...] He's being held captive on Taspir III. It's an Imperial controlled planet. a real paradise.|Kyle Katarn to Jaden Korr|<ref name="Jedi Academy" />}}
During the [[Disciples of Ragnos crisis|conflict]] with the [[Disciples of Ragnos]], a '''mission''' was carried out by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jaden Korr]] and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kyle Katarn]] in [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]].

==Background==
As the [[New Jedi Order]] was mobilizing all available manpower to intercept the [[Disciples of Ragnos]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], Kyle Katarn informed Jaden Korr that he had received a distress call from [[Rosh Penin]], who said he was being held captive on [[Taspir III]]. Korr was dubious because Penin had turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], and feared a trap, but he agreed to help Katarn nonetheless.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

==The mission==
The two Jedi arrived at an Imperial [[Taspir III power plant|smelting facility]] on the barren, lava-ridden surface of Taspir III. Katarn dropped Korr off at the back entrance of the plant and flew the ''[[Raven's Claw]]'' around to enter from another entrance. Korr proceeded through the complex, climbing the levels of the tall towers and crossing perilous bridges, avoiding deadly lava flows, dispatching scores of Imperial troops and [[Reborn|New Reborn]] along the way. Eventually, he encountered the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Alora (Dark Jedi)|Alora]], who taunted him and fled. Korr pursued her through the facility, navigating dangerous assembly lines and machining equipment. During the chase, Korr became increasingly frustrated and angry, treading perilously close to the dark side.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

Alora eventually led him to Penin, whom Korr confronted in his rage. Alora encouraged him to kill Penin, believing that if he could be turned he would be a powerful ally. Katarn, in another part of the plant, used the Force to communicate telepathically with Korr, telling him to resist temptation. Korr let go of his anger, deactivated his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], and asked Penin for forgiveness. Alora was angered, and engaged Korr in a duel, severing Penin's arm in the process. After minutes of intense fighting, Korr managed to defeat and kill Alora. Katarn arrived, and knew that Penin had to receive medical attention. He carried Penin away, unconscious, and praised Korr for making the right choice. The two Jedi parted there, and Korr went on to Korriban to assist the Jedi strike force in battling the Disciples of Ragnos and the Imperial Remnant.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'' game, the player has a choice between the light and dark sides of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. If the player chooses the non-canonical, dark side ending, Korr mortally wounds Penin, and then refuses to join the Disciples of Ragnos, slaying Alora as well. After Korr leaves, Katarn enters and finds Penin, who says he was sorry that both of Katarn's students had failed him. Before Penin dies, Katarn assures him that he had become a Jedi. He then leaves to pursue Korr.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'' {{1st}}
